title: Miconia norlindii
taxonomy: Miconia norlindii is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Miconia, which comprises over 1,000 species of plants. The family Melastomataceae is an order of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid families. The classification of Miconia norlindii places it within the order Myrtales, which also includes other families such as the Myrtaceae and the Combretaceae. This classification is based on phylogenetic studies and reflects the evolutionary relationships among these plant families.
uses: Miconia norlindii is a plant species found in Ecuador, but limited information is available regarding its specific medicinal uses.
